title: Push Pull
explanation: Forces push or pull objects making them start move stop speed up slow down or change direction or shape.
funFact: You use forces every time you kick ball or open door!

title: Friction Slow
prompt: Which surface will toy car travel furthest on after ramp?
explanation: Smooth surfaces have low friction so car keeps moving further. Rough surfaces have high friction slowing quickly.
funFact: Ice skaters glide because ice has very low friction!

title: High Friction
prompt: Which surface has most friction to stop quickly?
explanation: Rough surfaces grip more increasing friction slowing movement.
funFact: Sandpaper is used to increase friction to smooth wood by rubbing!

title: Magnetic Materials
prompt: Which materials are magnetic attract to magnet?
explanation: Only iron nickel cobalt and steel alloys containing iron are strongly magnetic.
funFact: Fridge magnets stick because fridge door has steel sheet inside!

title: Poles Repel
prompt: What happens when north pole faces north pole of another magnet?
explanation: Like poles repel, opposite poles attract. North-north repel, south-south repel, north-south attract.
funFact: Earth itself acts like giant magnet with north and south magnetic poles!

title: Poles Attract
explanation: Opposite poles attract each other pulling together.
funFact: Compass needle north points to Earth's magnetic north because opposite attract!

title: Gravity Down
prompt: Which force pulls everything down to Earth?
explanation: Gravity pulls objects towards centre of Earth giving weight. Without gravity we'd float away.
funFact: On Moon gravity is weaker so astronauts bounce higher!

title: Ramp Height
prompt: How to make toy car go faster down ramp?
explanation: Higher steeper ramp gives more gravitational potential energy converting to speed.
funFact: Roller coasters start high to gain speed for whole ride!

title: Contact vs Non Contact
prompt: Which is contact force needing touch?
explanation: Contact forces need touching surfaces push pull friction air resistance water resistance. Non-contact work at distance gravity magnetism.
funFact: You feel contact forces every step when shoe pushes ground!

title: Magnet Through Paper
prompt: Will magnet attract paperclip through thin card paper?

options: Yes magnetic force passes through thin non-magnetic materials

explanation: Magnetic field passes through paper plastic wood glass thin card but not thick steel sheet.
funFact: You can move paperclip under paper with magnet on top like magic trick!
